description:
Type halt-cont per program so the seam at chain-end matches what
flows in (user direction: "the halt 'continuation' passed in should
just match this type, and can be implemented by a regular function
in the runtime, appropriate to the type").

## Why bundled with 22.2

22.2 narrows cont seams (.21.1's three sites). After 22.2 lands, the
cont's Term::Return uses sig (my_return_repr, i64) — but the
hardcoded Tagged halt_cont_body sig at chain-end mismatches → UB.
So 22.5 MUST land with or before 22.2.

Failing tests at the boundary (verified by speculative .21.1 patch
without halt-cont widening): fact_5, fact_10, sum_list, add1_via_call,
const_int, float_const, binop_int, box_unbox, fst_snd, two_processes,
unop_neg, self_returns_task_pid, spawn_after_idle_resumes_progress,
spawn_enqueues_child_task. All produce wrong halt_value via
double/triple-tagging at the seam.

## Scope (atomic units)

### A. Runtime stubs (small, isolated)

runtime/src/ir_runtime.rs:
- fz_halt_implicit_i64(val: i64): current_process().halt_value = val
- fz_halt_implicit_f64(val: f64): halt_value = val.to_bits() as i64
- Update fz_get_halt_cont to take (addr: u64, kind: u32) -> u64;
  kind∈{0,1,2} selects which of three singleton slots to use.

### B. Process: 3-singleton table

runtime/src/process.rs:
- Replace `halt_cont_singleton: *mut u8` with
  `halt_cont_singletons: [*mut u8; 3]`.
- Replace `init_halt_cont_singleton(addr)` with
  `init_halt_cont_singletons(addrs: [*const u8; 3])`. Null slots
  stay null (lazy via fz_get_halt_cont).

### C. Codegen: three halt_cont_body fns

src/ir_codegen.rs:
- Declare halt_cont_body_{tagged,i64,f64}_id with sigs
  (Tagged i64 | i64 | f64, i64) -> i64 tail.
- Emit all three bodies; each calls matching halt_implicit_*.
- RuntimeRefs carries all three ids + halt_implicit_{i64,f64}_id.
- CompiledModule.halt_cont_body_addrs: [*const u8; 3]; make_process
  passes to init_halt_cont_singletons.

### D. fz_main_entry repr-aware

src/ir_codegen.rs:
- Defer fz_main_entry emission until after return_reprs is computed.
- Compute main_repr = return_reprs[main_spec_id].
- fz_main_entry uses get_halt_cont with kind = main_repr's index +
  the matching body addr.
- Sig of call_indirect to main is (main_repr.cl_type, ...) when
  main's own sig changes — actually main's sig stays (cont:i64) tail;
  the halt_cont closure +16 points at the right-typed body.

### E. fz_spawn_entry stays Tagged

Spawn'd closures are closure-target fns (forced Tagged return). Use
get_halt_cont(tagged_body_addr, kind=0).

### F. Synth halt-cont call sites (in compile_fn)

~5 sites in Term::Call / TailCall / Receive (search:
runtime.halt_cont_body_id). Each computes the callee's
return_repr; passes matching body_id + kind to get_halt_cont.

### G. AOT shim

runtime/src/aot_shim.rs:
- fz_aot_setup takes 3 body addrs instead of 1.
- aot_spawn_hook init_halt_cont_singletons with all 3 from the
  cached AOT_HALT_CONT_BODY_ADDRS (or pass to spawn hook).

src/ir_codegen.rs emit_aot_c_main:
- Updated setup_sig to take 3 i64 body addrs.
- emit_aot_c_main signature takes [FuncId; 3].
- Pass all three via call to fz_aot_setup.

### H. Tests + goldens

- cargo test --workspace passes.
- BLESS=1 cargo test golden_clif: add1.clif's ishl_imm/bor_imm at
  cont seam GONE; sshr_imm at cont entry GONE.
- Closure-bearing goldens (higher_order, closure_typed_captures,
  concurrency_ping_pong) may stay Tagged at the seam — closure-target
  return_repr is forced Tagged (separate ticket 22.6).

## Risks / open questions

- Does fz_main_entry's call_indirect sig need to match main's repr,
  or is main's sig always (cont:i64) tail? The cont:i64 is a closure
  pointer; the seam type only appears at Term::Return's
  call_indirect to the cont. So main's outer sig stays (cont:i64) tail.
  fz_main_entry just needs to pass the right-typed halt_cont closure.
- Same fn reachable from main (narrow) AND spawn (Tagged): the typer's
  per-spec narrowing keeps these distinct. If a single spec serves
  both, it widens to Tagged via the typer's fixpoint. .21.4 already
  handles transitive return propagation.
- One Process can be used for multiple runs (run_in). Singletons are
  per-Process; reset semantics needed? Per current code, singletons
  persist across runs of the same Process — fine, body addrs are
  module-static.
title: fz-cps-closure.5: typed halt-cont per program
